description:
A space startup is building an Earth Observation constellation that requires a strict 10:30 AM Sun-Synchronous Orbit (SSO). The baseline script at ~/Documents/missions/sso_baseline.script uses an analytical inclination guess of 97.787 deg, but under high-fidelity perturbations (10x10 gravity + lunar/solar), the Right Ascension of the Ascending Node (RAAN) drifts at the wrong rate. Modify the script to determine the precise inclination required to achieve a target RAAN (Sat.EarthMJ2000Eq.RAAN) of exactly 9.8565 degrees (tolerance 0.0001) after a 10-day propagation. Use the initial guess of 97.787 with a perturbation of 0.001 and a max step of 0.05 for the adjustment. Save the modified script and create a report at ~/GMAT_output/precise_sso_report.txt with exactly these lines:
converged_inclination_deg: <your_converged_value>
final_raan_deg: <your_final_raan_value>

Login: username=ga, password=password123
